steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What do you use to see?
What do you use to taste?
What do you use to hear?
questions.hint: You can wear earrings on them.

steps.title: My Happy Face
steps.text: This is my face. I have two big eyes. I have a small nose. My mouth is smiling. I am happy!

steps.audio_text: I have a happy face. My eyes are big and blue. My nose is small, and my mouth smiles.

questions.question_text: I have two ___ on my face. (Vocabulary question)
Complete the sentence: I have one ____? (Grammar question)
How many eyes are there on a face? (Reading comprehension question)
True or False: Your mouth is for seeing.
What do you use to smell? (Vocabulary question)

lessons.title: Listen and Look
lessons.topic: Auditory and decorative parts of the head

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What do we see with?
questions.hint: You have two of them on your face.

questions.question_text: What do we smell with?
questions.hint: It's in the middle of your face.

questions.question_text: What do we taste with?
questions.hint: You use it to eat and talk.

steps.title: My Funny Face
steps.text: Look! This is my funny face. I have small ears and big blue hair. My head is round. Can you hear me with your ears?

steps.explanation: Say what color a part is.

steps.audio_text: Hello! This is my head. I have two ears and brown hair.

questions.question_text: My hair is ____. (Fill in the color, Grammar question)
What do you listen with? (Vocabulary question)
What is on top of your head?
True or False: Your nose is for looking.

lessons.title: My Whole Head
lessons.topic: Reviewing the head as a whole

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What do you wear on your head to keep the sun away?
questions.hint: It has a round brim to block the sun.

questions.question_text: What helps you see better when it's bright outside?
questions.hint: You wear it on your eyes to make things clearer.

questions.question_text: What keeps your neck warm in chilly weather?
questions.hint: It's long and wraps around your neck.

steps.title: Big Smile, Small Nose
steps.text: I have a big smile. This is my nose. It is small. The elephant has big ears. They are very large!

questions.question: Is my nose big or small?

steps.explanation: Use big or small to talk about size.

steps.audio_text: This is my head. I have a small nose and big blue eyes. My ears are very small.

questions.question_text: What word describes something not big? (Vocabulary question)
Fill in the blank: I have ___ smile. (Grammar question)
Is the statement true? 'I have small ears.' means ears are not big.
Read and choose: 'My eyes are small.' What is being described?

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What part of your face helps you see?
What do we use to hear music?
questions.hint: They help you hear the birds sing!

questions.question_text: Which part moves when you talk?
questions.hint: You use it to kiss Mom and Dad!

steps.title: Funny Face
steps.text: This is my funny face! I have two big blue eyes. I have one small orange nose. My mouth is big and smiles a lot. Do you like my face?

questions.question: How many noses does it have?

steps.audio_text: This is my big, fluffy dog! He has two small eyes and a big, black nose. I love my dog's long ears.

questions.question: What color is the dog's nose?
How many ears does the dog have?

steps.prompt: Say: I have a big head and two small ears.
steps.expected_text: i have a big head and two small ears

questions.question_text: I ___ two big eyes. (Grammar question)
Which emoji shows 'nose'? (Vocabulary question)
True or False: 'This is my big teddy bear' uses an adjective.
Which is correct: This is my ___. (Reading Comprehension)
